anchors.definition: The additional risk premium embedded in asset prices due to active or escalating geopolitical conflict, sanctions, or military events.
anchors.domain_profile: Risk premium visible in commodity spreads and safe-haven demand

anchors.definition: A specific event, data release, or market signal that creates or reinforces a high-conviction short opportunity. Hard trigger, time-bounded.
anchors.domain_profile: Event-driven trigger for initiating or adding to short positions

anchors.definition: The prevailing bearish positioning bias in the market. Background condition, not a trigger. Requires [SH-C] to activate.
anchors.domain_profile: Background bearish condition — predisposes market to downside

anchors.definition: Current state of market liquidity and funding availability. Acts as multiplier on all other market signals.
anchors.domain_profile: Liquidity as amplifier/suppressor of market moves

anchors.definition: Borrow low-yield (JPY/CHF) to invest high-yield (EM/AUD). Sensitive to [LIQ] and [WAR]. Unwind = sharp reversal.
Systemic fragility from correlated position concentration. High [STACK] + tight [LIQ] = violent unwind risk.
anchors.domain_profile: Crowding risk — systemic fragility from position concentration
